nodes.name: devices.json
nodes.summary: Stores the machine-local list of devices that recently opened the Expo project. The current development-session list is empty.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Explains why Expo creates the machine-specific .expo directory, what its generated files contain, and why the directory should not be committed.

nodes.name: .understandignore
nodes.summary: Provides commented ignore-pattern examples for excluding tests and generated directories from Understand Anything analysis. No optional project-specific exclusion pattern is currently enabled.

nodes.name: config.json
nodes.summary: Configures Understand Anything to generate project-analysis output in English.

nodes.name: village_demo_01.json
nodes.summary: Defines the generated schema-v3 Village Demo indoor map, including a 70-by-60 tile floor, 20 assets, 449 visual layers, collision data, spawn and display metadata, and a four-node route graph.
nodes.languageNotes: The JSON combines rendering, collision, navigation, and movement-oriented map representations in one versioned schema.

nodes.name: .gitkeep
nodes.summary: Preserves the components directory in version control when it contains no other files.

nodes.name: .gitkeep
nodes.summary: Preserves the hooks directory in version control when it contains no other files.

nodes.name: .gitkeep
nodes.summary: Preserves the movement algorithms directory in version control independently of its implementation files.

nodes.name: particle-filter-layer-readme-zh.md
nodes.summary: Documents the particle-filter layer from particle creation through prediction, scoring, systematic resampling, and weighted position estimation. It emphasizes that the current implementation smooths PDR motion without yet applying map constraints.
nodes.languageNotes: The source is primarily Chinese with TypeScript identifiers and English indoor-positioning terminology.

nodes.name: .gitkeep
nodes.summary: Preserves the movement constraints directory in version control independently of its implementation files.

nodes.name: .gitkeep
nodes.summary: Preserves the movement estimate directory in version control independently of its implementation files.

nodes.name: estimate-pdr-layer-readme-zh.md
nodes.summary: Explains how the PDR estimate layer converts cleaned sensor inputs into step, heading, displacement, and combined motion estimates. It defines the boundary between raw-sensor preprocessing, particle filtering, and later map constraints.
nodes.languageNotes: The source is primarily Chinese and uses formulas and TypeScript data-contract names to explain the PDR pipeline.

nodes.name: MOVEMENT_SYSTEM_README.md
nodes.summary: Defines the movement system architecture that turns phone sensor data into stable indoor positions through collection, preprocessing, estimation, algorithms, constraints, and orchestration. It also establishes strict boundaries with collision, actor, renderer, and camera subsystems.

nodes.name: .gitkeep
nodes.summary: Preserves the sensor preprocessing directory in version control independently of its implementation files.

nodes.name: sensor-preprocessing-readme-zh.md
nodes.summary: Documents sensor-sample normalization and fixed-capacity time windows used to provide safe, ordered input to movement estimation. It covers fallback handling for invalid numeric values and sensor-specific cleanup without performing position estimation itself.
nodes.languageNotes: The source is primarily Chinese with TypeScript examples for discriminated sensor samples and immutable window operations.

nodes.name: .gitkeep
nodes.summary: Preserves the raw sensor collection directory in version control independently of its implementation files.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Defines ownership boundaries among the map engine's controller, composition view, rendering, actor, movement, and camera subsystems. It directs screens through the public controller surface and prevents rendering or state responsibilities from leaking across subsystem folders.

nodes.name: .gitkeep
nodes.summary: Preserves the navigation directory in version control when it contains no other files.

nodes.name: .gitkeep
nodes.summary: Preserves the screens directory in version control independently of its implemented screen components.

nodes.name: .gitkeep
nodes.summary: Preserves the services directory in version control when it contains no other files.

nodes.name: .gitkeep
nodes.summary: Preserves the storage directory in version control alongside stored map assets.

nodes.name: map.json
nodes.summary: Provides the runtime copy of the schema-v3 Village Demo indoor map consumed by the application, with visual and collision layers, asset metadata, spawn configuration, labels, icons, and a four-node navigation graph.
nodes.languageNotes: This file is byte-identical to generated_map/village_demo_01.json and serves as the application-facing stored map asset.

nodes.name: .gitkeep
nodes.summary: Preserves the application state-store directory in version control when it contains no other files.

nodes.name: .gitkeep
nodes.summary: Preserves the shared types directory in version control when it contains no other files.
